discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape led by semis (SMH +4%) on apparent AVGO/AAPL strength, while biotech (XBI -4.4%) and communication services (XLC -1.8%, GOOGL -3.9%) lagged sharply. Mid-cap movers cluster cleanly along these sector lines: semi-cap equipment names ripping with SMH, biotech names bleeding with XBI.

notable_index_moves: SMH +4.01% — semi-cap equipment and chip names broadly bid, likely AVGO-led (+4.7%)
XBI -4.39% — sharp biotech selloff against an otherwise green tape
XLC -1.76% on GOOGL -3.86% — communication services dragged by mega-cap
MSFT -4.17% despite QQQ +0.46% — idiosyncratic, possibly Build/AI announcement digestion
VIX -1.74% to 15.77 — risk appetite intact at index level

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment sympathy rally with sector ETF
discoveries.thesis: ONTO ripping alongside SMH +4% and AVGO +4.7% with no company-specific 8-K. Move magnitude is roughly 2x the sector ETF, which is consistent with higher-beta semi-cap equipment names that typically amplify SMH moves. Nothing on the tape suggests this is overdone or underdone — it's a beta trade.
discoveries.what_confirms: Peer semi-cap names (KLAC, LRCX, AMAT) showing similar 2x-SMH beta on the day; no ONTO-specific news surfacing in coming sessions
discoveries.what_kills: An ONTO-specific catalyst (analyst upgrade with raised PT, contract win, M&A chatter) emerging post-close — would reclassify as UNDERDONE if magnitude exceeded news
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with SMH/AVGO semi rally; no 8-K filed
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + SMH +4% + AVGO +4.7% on the same day = sector beta trade
research_pointers: Check if AVGO earnings/guidance overnight or analyst day drove the SMH move and how ONTO's metrology exposure ties to that
Compare ONTO's move vs KLAC/CAMT (closer metrology peers) — divergence would flag idiosyncratic factor

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap-adjacent rebound after prior week weakness
discoveries.thesis: AEIS up sharply but five-day still negative, suggesting today is a recovery within a downtrend driven by the broader semi-cap rally (SMH +4%). Magnitude reasonable given the bounce-back math and beta. No catalyst signals.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued mean-reversion in semi-cap names through the week; no negative AEIS-specific news emerging
discoveries.what_kills: AEIS-specific announcement (guidance reset, customer concentration disclosure) emerging — would force reclassification
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move tracks SMH direction with amplified magnitude after a down week
research_pointers: Identify what caused the prior multi-day decline — if it was a guide cut, today's bounce is just dead-cat
Check AEIS exposure to data center power (industrial classification but heavily semi-equipment levered)

discoveries.name: PTC Therapeutics
discoveries.setup: Biotech selloff on broad XBI weakness with no name-specific catalyst
discoveries.thesis: PTCT down hard but five-day is positive and volume is BELOW average (0.75x). XBI -4.4% explains roughly half the move, leaving a residual that could be idiosyncratic or simply beta-amplified. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no headline visible. Cannot articulate either a bull (oversold bounce) or bear (specific catalyst) case at conf 3+ without knowing what dragged XBI.
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with XBI biotech selloff; no 8-K
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + XBI -4.39% on the same day + below-average volume on PTCT
research_pointers: Identify the XBI catalyst — was there a sector-wide policy headline (drug pricing, FDA)?
Check PTCT's near-term pipeline calendar (Duchenne/Huntington readouts) — any de-risking ahead of data?

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics
discoveries.setup: High-beta gene editing name down with XBI biotech rout
discoveries.thesis: BEAM -7% on a day XBI is -4.4% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap pre-revenue gene editing name. Volume is below average (0.87x), arguing against panic selling. Magnitude appears to match sector pressure rather than name-specific bad news.
discoveries.what_confirms: CRSP also down 4% on the same day (in the mover list) — confirms gene editing basket pressure rather than BEAM-specific issue
discoveries.what_kills: An overnight 8-K on a pipeline setback or partnership termination would reclassify as UNDERDONE
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + CRSP -4% same day + XBI -4.4% = basket move
research_pointers: Find the XBI catalyst — drug pricing executive order, FDA news, or sector ETF outflow
Compare BEAM/CRSP/NTLA moves — divergence would flag idiosyncratic risk
discoveries.what_to_learn: When two names in a thematic basket (BEAM + CRSP, both gene editing) move similarly on a day the sector ETF moves sharply, the default explanation is sector flow, not company news. Saves the analyst from inventing catalysts.

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Semi-cap equipment rally — SMH +4%, AVGO +4.7%, TSM +2.5%

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump signed scaled-back AI executive order asking companies to voluntarily share frontier models with government 30 days pre-release
trump_signals.note: Critics call it 'almost meaningless' — voluntary and narrow. Unlikely to be the main driver of MSFT -4.2% or GOOGL -3.9% today; those moves more likely tied to Build conference reactions and competitive AI dynamics. Worth flagging the order exists, but low signal for tradeable mid-cap mispricings.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump rhetoric on Iran surrender/war, naval blockade language from Gingrich repost
trump_signals.note: XLE +1.15% consistent with mild geopolitical premium but VIX down to 15.77 suggests market is discounting hot-war scenario. Not actionable for mid-caps in tonight's mover list.

discovery.no_signals_note: Movers tonight are dominated by two clean sector flows (semis up, biotech down) with no 8-Ks attached to any name. This is a low-conviction discovery night for stock-specific mispricings — the right call on most of these is RATIONAL sympathy. The real catalyst worth Michael identifying is what drove XBI -4.4% on a green tape; that's where any mid-cap biotech mispricing lives, not in the individual names listed.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI launches Codex tools for white-collar work (data analytics, creative, sales, product design, equity investing, investment banking)
ai_announcements.summary: Six job-specific Codex plug-ins targeting white-collar workflows. Direct overlap with horizontal SaaS and specialized analytics/research tools, but none of the moving stocks in the data set are clear targets.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
ai_announcements.summary: ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL (flag for bias grading). Project Glasswing expansion to 150 orgs in power, water, healthcare, communications. Implies AI-driven vulnerability discovery at scale; bullish for cybersecurity spend but no cybersecurity names in today's movers.

ai_announcements.headline: Alphabet to raise $80B for AI buildout citing supply-constrained demand
ai_announcements.summary: Massive capex commitment reinforces AI infrastructure demand thesis. Plausible read-through to semicap equipment names that supply advanced-node and HBM/packaging tools.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto's metrology/inspection tools sell into HBM packaging and advanced logic; hyperscaler capex acceleration is a direct tailwind, not a threat. Move is bullish sympathy.
research_pointers: Check if ONTO had company-specific news (analyst upgrade, contract win) — 1.54x volume on +8.7% suggests possibly more than just sympathy
HBM exposure mix vs advanced logic; Alphabet capex skews to TPU/custom silicon
Compare move vs KLAC/AMAT/LRCX peers today

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco's laser annealing and ion beam tools support advanced node and HBM; AI capex cycle is positive. Low volume multiple (0.51x) suggests sympathy move not a fundamental catalyst.
research_pointers: Veeco's exposure to AI-relevant end markets vs legacy

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy supplies power conversion to semicap and data center customers — directly levered to AI buildout. Recovers from -8% prior 5-day move, so partly mean-reversion plus AI capex sentiment.
research_pointers: Data center power exposure as % of revenue
Read-through from Alphabet $80B — power infrastructure is a key constraint
Was -8% five-day drop on company-specific news that's now reversing?

ai_announcements.headline: Microsoft launches Scout (OpenClaw-inspired assistant) and AI agent control/eval tooling at Build
ai_announcements.summary: Microsoft 365 personal assistant plus dev tools for agent policy and evals. Overlap with horizontal AI assistants and eval startups; no clear public-stock victim in today's movers.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Biotech selloffs (PTCT -7.55%, BEAM -7.0%, CRSP -4.0%) are unrelated to AI news flow — almost certainly sector/clinical-data driven; excluded. EXTR +4.8% has no clean AI catalyst tie in today's news. Main actionable thread is semicap/power sympathy with Alphabet's $80B AI capex raise.
